qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via GroovyShell.evaluate() in matrix exclude filter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While this finding targets the same Jervis project (net.gleske:jervis), the advisory root cause is deterministic AES IV derivation in SecurityIO.groovy:866-874 and :891-900 (CWE-327). This finding describes code injection via unsanitized string interpolation in LifecycleGenerator.matrixExcludeFilterBuilder() and GroovyShell.evaluate() in MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy:526. Different vulnerable function, file, and root cause mechanism entirely.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes deterministic AES IV derivation from passphrase in SecurityIO.groovy (CWE-327). No qualifying finding identifies this vulnerable code or root cause. The 'groovyshell-code-injection-via-matrix-exclude' finding targets the same Jervis project but describes a completely different vulnerability (code injection via GroovyShell.evaluate() in LifecycleGenerator/MultiPlatformGenerator).
evidence: Advisory references SecurityIO.groovy:866-874 and :891-900 for deterministic AES IV derivation.
Advisory patch commit c3981ff71de7b0f767dfe7b37a2372cb2a51974a addresses random IV generation in encrypt/decrypt methods.
No finding references SecurityIO.groovy, encrypt/decrypt methods, AES IV generation, or CWE-327.
